entry.title: Insert a New Row into table at a specific Index using Odata v4
entry.content:
Hi Forum,

I have an issue , Please help me in resolving it!!!

When I am trying to create a row with the following code:

// Get the list binding from the table's "items" aggregation
var oListBinding = oTable.getBinding("items");
var oInitialData = {
"site": "1000",
"storage_location":"1234",
"article_doc_num": "1234",
"article_doc_num_itm":"1"
};
// Create a new entry (context)
var oNewContext = oListBinding.create(oInitialData);

The new row is getting created at the beginning of the table, but I want the row to be added at a specific index.


Please suggest a solution for this.

entry.title: Unable to Deploy Joules Skills in SAP BPA - ClientNotFound
entry.content:
Hello, I have an error when I try to deploy or test Joule in SAP BPA.

"The project cannot be deployed by the component "Integration Gateway". Error while trying to connect to Joule - JouleClientService - invalid_client - Client Not Found"

I have the roles for das-application but it still doesn't reaches. Also, when I try to test, the environments don't show up. 

Here some examples:

entry.title: Can we extract Course Completion Certificate via API in SAP SuccessFactors LMS?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I would like to understand whether it is possible to extract or download Course Completion Certificates via API in SAP SuccessFactors LMS.

Business Requirement:

When a user completes a course, a Certificate of Completion is generated in LMS.

We want to fetch this certificate (PDF or file format) using an API and integrate it with an external system.

Questions:

Is there any standard LMS API or OData API available to download completion certificates?

Can certificates be accessed via Plateau Web Services (SOAP APIs) or any other integration method?

If direct extraction is not supported, what are the recommended alternatives (e.g., document management, custom solution, report-based approach)?

Any guidance, documentation references, or real-time implementation experience would be highly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: OCN OData API - "Error adding OCN course" for ALL providers including GO1 on partner demo tenant
entry.content:
## Environment

 

- Tenant: sfcpart002659 (Partner Demo)

- API Endpoint: /learning/odatav4/public/admin/ocn/v1/OcnCourses

 

## Issue

 

POST requests to create OCN courses fail with a generic error for ALL providers - both custom (DLA) and built-in (GO1):

 

{"error":{"code":null,"message":"Error adding OCN course - test-go1-001"}}

 

The operationStatusDetail=true parameter does not return additional details.

 

## What Works

 

- OAuth token generation via /learning/oauth-api/rest/v1/token

- GET requests to $metadata and OcnCourses

- Provider configuration visible in Content > Open Content Network

 

## What We Configured

 

1. Created provider "DLA" in OPEN_CONTENT_NETWORK system configuration

2. Created label label.u.DLA with proper locale value

3. Created admin user with OCN_ADMIN role (with "Open Content Network Import" permission)

4. DLA appears correctly as "Data Literacy Academy (DLA)" in Content Providers list

 

## Testing Performed

 

| Provider | Payload | Result |

|----------|---------|--------|

| DLA | Full payload with launchType 4 | Error adding OCN course |

| DLA | Minimal payload | Error adding OCN course |

| GO1 | Minimal payload | Error adding OCN course |

 

## Questions

 

1. Is there additional tenant-level configuration required to enable OCN course imports via OData API?

2. Are partner demo tenants restricted from using the OCN POST API?

3. What could cause this error for built-in providers like GO1?

entry.title: Add Value Help to field ProductForEdit in Fiori App Manage Product Master (F1602)
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am trying to add an additional value to the “Product” field (ProductForEdit) in C_Product and then use it in the Fiori app “Manage Product Master (F1602)”. But it is not displayed in the app. I suspect that the MD_C_PRODUCT_MAINTAIN_SRV service is doing something (such as hard-coding value helps in _mpc – although I can't find them...) to override the annotations.

Here are all possible value helps for the “Product” field (/ProductForEdit):



My metadata extension to C_Product:

@Metadata.layer: #CORE
annotate entity C_Product with
{
  @Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [ { entity: { name: 'I_ProductStdVH', element: 'Product' },
                                        label: 'Products by Description' },
                                      { entity: { name: 'I_ProductByPlantVH', element: 'Product' },
                                        qualifier: 'ProdPlant',
                                        label: 'Products by Plant' },
                                      { entity: { name: 'I_ProductByDistributionChainVH', element: 'Product' },
                                        qualifier: 'ProdDistrChain',
                                        label: 'Products by Distribution Chain' },
                                      { entity: { name: 'I_ProdByEWMWarehouseVH', element: 'Product' },
                                        qualifier: 'ProdEwm',
                                        label: 'Products by EWM Warehouse' },
                                      { entity: { name: 'I_ProductByChangeMasterVH', element: 'Product' },
                                        qualifier: 'ProdChangeMaster',
                                        label: 'Products by Change Number' },
                                      { entity: { name: 'I_ProductByValuationVH', element: 'Product' },
                                        qualifier: 'ProdValuation',
                                        label: 'Products by Valuation' },
                                      { entity: { name: 'ZI_ProductByMatchcodeVH', element: 'matnr' },
                                        qualifier: 'ProdMatch',
                                        label: 'Products by Matchcode' } ]
  ProductForEdit;
}

Here, I simply copied the annotation Consumption.valueHelpDefinition from C_Product and added my own value help. I can see that I am in the right place because when I remove some of the standard value helps, they are also removed from the app. However, my addition is not displayed.

When I create my own service based on c_product (and preview the app), I can see all value helps, including my own – so the code/annotations are correct. It just doesn't work via the MD_C_PRODUCT_MAINTAIN_SRV service.

Does anyone have any idea how to solve this problem?

Many thanks,
Janik

entry.title: Error in OData call made with self sign certificate
entry.content:
Hello All,

We want to establish a connection between a Python and SAP through OData call. For this, we are using a self signed X.509 certificate which we generated using OpenSSL. 

when we make a Odata call [which is based on a BW query], we are getting a 404 error with description : Please select a valid URL. If it is a valid URL, check whether service /sap/opu/odata/sap/<service_name>/$metadata is active in transaction SICF. 

we have set the odata Logon detail to SSL Certificate.

could you tell where we could be going wrong and how we can fix it?

OData SAP BW/4HANA Python

entry.title: Package Change and ProcessingMode Unification for Standard OData Service
entry.content:
We would like to request your guidance on the recommended procedures for changing the package and unifying the ProcessingMode of the standard OData service API_PROD_ORDER_CONFIRMATION_2_SRV.

[Current Situation]

  • Target OData Service: API_PROD_ORDER_CONFIRMATION_2_SRV (Standard Service)
  • Package Inconsistency: Some objects of this OData service are currently assigned to the $TMP package (only on the development system).
  • ProcessingMode Discrepancy: The ProcessingMode for API_PROD_ORDER_CONFIRMATION_2_SRV is not consistent across our environments (Development, Test, Staging, Production). Specifically, it is Routing-based only on the Development system.
  • Note: Currently, no system errors or service operation issues have arisen due to these inconsistencies. However, we aim to unify these settings across all our systems.

[Questions/Consultation Items]

  1. Standard OData Service Package Change: Is changing the package of objects belonging to a standard OData service like API_PROD_ORDER_CONFIRMATION_2_SRV a recommended practice by SAP?

    • If not recommended, could you suggest alternative approaches (e.g., using a custom service, applying an SAP Note fix)?
    • If recommended or unavoidable, please provide specific guidance on the procedure (e.g., how to ensure no objects are missed, potential risks, points to consider).

  2. ProcessingMode Unification: To unify the ProcessingMode to Co-deployed only, are there any considerations or specific steps beyond the package change that we should be aware of?

  3. Object Identification: Could you recommend the most reliable methods or tools to comprehensively identify all related objects for this OData service? (We are currently investigating using transactions like /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE, /n/IWBEP/REG_MODEL, /n/IWBEP/REG_SERVICE, SEGW, SE24, and SICF.)

We appreciate your time and kind assistance in this matter.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: About OData API
entry.content:
Hi,

I am learning about [Hands-on Practice for SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Administration].
I created a new employee and want to  get the employee data via OData API, But I don't find out the OData API.

Who can help me?

entry.title: ODATA_CALLING_ERROR: How to configure Resource Path in OData Sender Adapter
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are working on an OData to JDBC integration where we are trying to fetch IDoc status information from SAP and update it on the client side.

This is a CDS view with parameters exposed as ODATA which we are trying to consume from ODATA Sender channel.
We have created an HTTP Destination as shown below:

Destination Name: ZCAD_I_IDOC_STATUS
URL:
https://agvdwdci01.corp.mooginc.com/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZCAD_I_IDOC_STATUS_CDS?sap-client=250&sap-language=EN

Resource Path : ZCAD_I_IDOC_STATUS(p_days=7)/Set
OData Sender Adapter and OData structure snapshots are attached for reference.

However, we are encountering the following error in the PO channel:

ODATA_CALLING_ERROR: OData call failed
404 : NOT_FOUND [ZCAD_I_IDOC_STATUS(p_days=7)/Set]

The same destination works correctly when tested directly in the browser as well as via Postman.

Could someone please advise what the correct Resource Path should be in the OData channel configuration to make this work successfully?

Any guidance or suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Custom fields added to extended SEGW OData entity not available in metadata
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

In Fiori App ID: F0843 (Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document), we are trying to add some custom fields in entity GR4PO_DL_Item of OData service MMIM_GR4PO_DL_SRV by redefinition and importing properties that are already available as an .include in the line item type MMIM_GR4PO_DL_ITEM.

After this we redefined "Define" method of MPC_EXT class with code:

  METHOD define.

  super->define( ).

  DATA(lo_entity_type) = model->get_entity_type( iv_entity_name = 'GR4PO_DL_Item' ).

  " Expand the specific include structure to expose its fields
  lo_entity_type->add_auto_expand_include(
    EXPORTING
      iv_include_name     = 'MMIM_GR4PO_DL_ITEM'
      iv_bind_conversions = abap_true
  ).
ENDMETHOD.

We followed the same approach as shown in this learning journey:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/building-odata-services-with-sap-gateway/redefining-an-sap-gateway-service

We already generated the Runtime objects and there seems to be no error as metadata loading properly. However, in the metadata the custom fields are not available and we are not sure what could be missing. The metadata does show the all the inlcudes but does not show the fields added to include, even though they are available in extended entity type.

Did someone face similar situation and can help in finding the solution?

entry.title: Report Attachment data in C4C v1
entry.content:
We have an attachment functionality in customers. We want to create a report based on this attachment. 

I don't think it's possible in the current data sources we have in C4C. I can only see OData attachments. So am I correct in assuming that only 3rd party reporting tool like PowerBI or SAC can accommodate this requirement via OData extraction?

Cheers,

Marc

entry.title: How to activate OData API on SAP ECC R3
entry.content:
Hello SAP Specialists

We want to use API_ATTACHMENT_SRV on SAP ECC, but so far on our SAP ECC system no OData API has been activated or used so far. primary mode of interface so far are IDocs, files, BAPIs for this system.

Since OData API is to be activated which has never been done so far on this system, could you please help me with the pre-requisites?

SAP NetWeaver Gateway is available on the system, software component SAP_GWFND too is available on the system on the list of installed software.

 

Regards,

GP

entry.title: Purging a Recruiting multiattachmentselection field (Candidate Profile)
entry.content:
Hello,

I am working on a Recruiting scenario where external candidates upload a photo into a multiattachmentselection field on the Candidate Profile.
For compliance purposes, this photo must be deleted after its use.

What I have verified so far

  • The attachment is stored via the standard Attachment entity (because the field type is multiattachmentselection in the Recruiting Candidate Profile).
  • Based on SAP’s documentation on Strategies for Purging Attachments, only the following attachment types support independent purge:
    • DRTM Onboarding attachments
    • MDF attachment fields
    • Auto Data Purge–eligible attachments
      All other types of attachments can only be purged together with their module-level purge options, not individually.
      Source: SAP Help — Strategies for Purging Attachments [help.sap.com]

Given this, it seems that DRM/DRTM cannot target a single multiattachmentselection field in Recruiting.
Using DRM would purge or anonymize the entire Candidate Profile or Application, not just the photo.

My questions:

  1. Is there any way to make a Recruiting attachment (multiattachmentselection field) purgeable through DRTM?

  2. Can a custom MDF object be used to store and purge such documents, or is this not recommended for Recruiting attachments?
    (It seems that copying the file into MDF would not help, because the original Recruiting attachment would still remain and need to be deleted manually or through API.)

  3. Is SAP’s recommended approach in this scenario to use Integration Center (or OData API DELETE) to selectively remove attachments from multiattachmentselection fields?

Any insights or confirmation from the community would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you !

entry.title: Odata V4 Virus Scan Issue
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

 

We are seeing an issue in Odata V4 calls to the backend system PSD, the error message we get is Virus scan profile /IWBEP/V4/ODATA_UPLOAD is not active 

We checked below SAP Notes which mentions to activate the profile in VSCANPROFILE transaction.

https://me.sap.com/notes/3634529/E

https://me.sap.com/notes/3027559

However what we want to understand is

  1. What impact it has on the ODATA V4 calls incoming to the system if we select SCANALLFILES scan configuration parameter while activating this profile ?
  2. What is the performance, usage addition on the system overall with having this profile activated on the system, as there may be countless such incomings calls throughout the business day? Considering if we select SCANALLFILES scan configuration parameter while activating this profile ?
  3. Is there any specific settings, recommendations from SAP ?

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Error -1796 on CREATE OR REPLACE ODATA PRODUCER
entry.content:
We use OData in our database. Occasionally, I receive an error message
Modify producer Not yet supported SQLCODE=-1796
when I execute the command CREATE OR REPLACE ODATA PRODUCER “odataxxx”.
This error occurs always in another database that uses mirroring.
What could be the cause?

DB V17 latest EBF

entry.title: PATCH operation failed on API_SALES_ORDER_SRV
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I am going to update two fields one from header i.e "PurchaseOrderByCustomer"  and another from Item "RequestedQuantity".
These two fields available in A_SalesOrderItem EntitySet. But when I am doing PATCH operation only "RequestedQuantity" is updating not "PurchaseOrderByCustomer". 

I am using  url 
/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_SALES_ORDER_SRV/A_SalesOrderItem(SalesOrder='7000000223',SalesOrderItem='0010')

{

    "PurchaseOrderByCustomer": "Alex",

    "RequestedQuantity": "20"

}

Is it not possible to update header field from item ? Is $batch operation work on this API ?

Can you please help me on this?

entry.title: how to trigger handler class read method in RAP OData V4 Web API?
entry.content:
Dear Colleagues,

Good day.

Well, I have a requirement, existing material data coming from third party system to CPI to S/4 HANA MDG and need to create change request and approve it.

So created a RAP service, Table , CDS view , service definition with binding type OData V4 Web API along with behavior definition/implementation In the handler class, defined the methods modify and read. I just want to see if read method is called and data filled. So i would like to know how i can execute/debug the handler class read method.

How can i do the GET call using GW client?

Service URL :  /sap/opu/odata4/sap/xxx_xx_xxx_sb/srvd_a2x/sap/xxxx_xx_xxxx_s/0001/

xxx_xx_xxx_sb -  Service binding         xxxx_xx_xxxx_s  - Service definition

 

 

 

regards

Chandra Kishore.

entry.title: Update custom SuccessFactors MDF field once workflow has been approved or sent back
entry.content:
Hi all

I have a custom MDF field that I would like to update based on the outcome of the workflow (either approved or send back). I have tried to configure business rules for the custom mdf object under "save rules" on the custom mdf object page, but it seems the status gets changed immediately as a new record is created and before the workflow gets approved, even though Pending data is set to "Yes".

I have created a new business rule by choosing Metadata framework > Rules for MDF objects and filling out the fields below:

I have also tried using the different options under purpose, but none seem to give me the correct options I need when structuring my IF statement (e.g. When workflow onsave = approve then status.value = approved etc.) 

Is there any possible workaround/correct solution to achieve this?

entry.title: OData V4 URL Encoding Issue with SAP Web Dispatcher + Proxy (Sales Order Management App)
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

We are facing an issue with OData V4 URL encoding after activating the standard Sales Order Management application in S/4HANA.

Note: An SAP incident has already been created, and in parallel we are reaching out to the community to learn from experts who may have faced a similar issue.

Issue summary

The problem occurs because equal signs (=) in the OData V4 request URL are being URL-encoded (%3D) before the request reaches the S/4HANA ICM.

In the browser, the request URL is correct and the = signs are not encoded:

https://etgwdsp.etgworld.com/sap/opu/odata4/sap/c_salesordermanage_srv/srvd_f4/sap/c_orgdivisionvaluehelp/0001;ps='srvd-c_salesordermanage_sd-0001';va='com.sap.gateway.srvd.c_salesordermanage_sd.v0001.ae-c_salesordermanage.createwithsalesordertype.organizationdivision.SalesOrderManageType.X'/$metadata?sap-language=EN

However, when the request reaches the ICM, the raw HTTP request shows that:

  • = is encoded as %3D

  • %27 is converted back to '

Example from ICM level 3 trace:

GET /sap/opu/odata4/.../0001;ps%3D'srvd-c_salesordermanage_sd-0001';va%3D'com.sap.gateway.srvd.c_salesordermanage_sd.v0001.ae-c_salesordermanage.createwithsalesordertype.organizationdivision.SalesOrderManageType.X'/$metadata?sap-language=EN

The SAP Gateway expects the “=” characters to remain unencoded, and because of this encoding, the request fails.

Landscape and behavior

Middleware involved

  • SAP Web Dispatcher

  • Corporate HTTP Proxy

Observed behavior

  1. All apps work when the proxy is bypassed (Web Dispatcher active)

  2. All apps work when the Web Dispatcher is bypassed (proxy active)

  3. The issue occurs only when both proxy and Web Dispatcher are active

This strongly indicates that the URL is being modified due to an interaction between the proxy and Web Dispatcher.

Troubleshooting performed

  • All required roles, authorizations, services, and ICF nodes are active

  • Web Dispatcher profile parameters adjusted to prevent URL encoding

  • Web Dispatcher mod file changes attempted for OData V4 handling
    → Issue still persists

As per internal network team analysis:

  • A change may be required in the Web Dispatcher index file

  • Neither the network team nor BASIS team has access to modify it

  • Only SAP can make changes at this level

Questions to the community

  • Has anyone experienced a similar OData V4 URL encoding issue with Web Dispatcher + proxy?

  • Is this a known limitation or defect in SAP Web Dispatcher for OData V4?

  • Are there any supported parameters, SAP Notes, or workarounds to prevent encoding of = in the URL?

  • Should this be handled via ICM settings, Web Dispatcher configuration, or proxy rules?

  • Is there any recommended architectural workaround until SAP provides a fix?

Any guidance, experiences, or references would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance for your support.

Varsha J S

 

#SAP #S4HANA #ODataV4 #SAPGateway #SAPWebDispatcher #ICM #SAPBasis #SAPFiori #SAPCommunity
@SAP @SAPCommunity @SAPSupport

entry.title: Guidance on best adapter choice for inbound integration to SAP Cloud Private via CPI
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I am working on an inbound integration scenario using SAP Cloud Integration.
An external (non-SAP) application will send data through CPI to SAP Cloud Private, mainly for creating and updating business objects.

From a design perspective, I am currently leaning toward OData services because they are standardized, REST-based, support CRUD operations, and are easier for future consumers such as web or mobile applications.

However, I would like to validate this assumption against real project experience and community best practices.

I would appreciate your advice on the following:

  1. In similar inbound scenarios, which adapter is most commonly recommended?

    • OData

    • IDoc

    • RFC/BAPI

    • SOAP

    • other approaches?

  2. Under what conditions would you recommend not using OData, even if the requirement is mainly CRUD?

  3. Looking at performance, monitoring, extensibility, and long-term maintenance, which option tends to be the most sustainable?

  4. If the backend is built using modern paradigms such as RAP/CDS, does that naturally position OData as the primary interface?

I am open to alternative recommendations if other approaches are considered more suitable.

Thank you very much for sharing your experience.
